title: Goals, Context, Outputs, Boundaries, and Done Conditions · Regression Repair Bay
summary: Write a production-ready prompt for a high-impact code and document change. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a reusable goal-context-output-boundary template and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State the desired result and audience before prescribing implementation steps.
Provide context that can change the result and route the agent to authoritative sources.
Specify output structure, granularity, evidence, and machine-readable constraints when they matter.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-done-conditions-igs7xz" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Done Conditions into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-done-conditions-igs7xz
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Done Conditions applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define observable completion checks including tests, review, and unresolved-evidence reporting.
